title: The Patch Master
subtitle: Examine commits, cherry-pick the good ones, skip the junk

briefing.narrative:
Two feature branches arrive for code review. Each has 4 commits. But the authors were... undisciplined.

Branch 'feature-search' has 4 commits: a working search implementation, a WIP experiment, a search optimization, and another experiment that broke things. Branch 'feature-export' has 4 commits: an export module, a debugging hack, a CSV formatter, and more debugging cruft.

The Head Monk slides a release checklist across the table. "We need a release branch with ONLY the good commits. Two from search, two from export. The experiments and debugging hacks stay behind."

He pauses. "But I'm not telling you WHICH commits are good. You'll need to examine each one with git log and git show. Read the commit messages. Look at the changes. Make your own judgment."

The cat has already reviewed both branches and is judging you silently.
briefing.concept:
In real codebases, you often need to selectively pick commits from branches that contain a mix of good work and throwaway experiments. Use git log to see commit messages, git show to inspect changes, then cherry-pick only what belongs in your release.

git log <branch> — see what commits exist
git show <ref> — inspect a specific commit
git cherry-pick <ref> — apply a commit to current branch
objectives: Examine both feature branches to identify the good commits
Cherry-pick ONLY the good commits: the working implementations and improvements, not the WIP/debug ones
The release branch should have search.txt and export.txt with the correct final content

args.content:
search module
index: built
query: parsed

WIP trying elasticsearch
this does not work yet

search module
index: built
query: parsed
cache: enabled
results: paginated

args.message: Optimize search with caching and pagination

args.content:
export module
format: json
streaming: enabled

console.log everywhere
debugging hack
remove before merge

args.message: DEBUG: add logging everywhere (REMOVE BEFORE MERGE)

args.content:
export module
format: json, csv
streaming: enabled
batch: supported

args.message: Add CSV format and batch export

hints.text: Use git log feature-search and git log feature-export to see commit messages. The WIP and DEBUG commits are the bad ones.

Create a release branch. Cherry-pick the 'Implement search' and 'Optimize search' commits from feature-search. Cherry-pick the 'Implement export' and 'Add CSV' commits from feature-export. Skip all WIP and DEBUG commits.
